rulings.Case Title: EDWARD W. SCOTT ELECTRIC CO., INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. THOMPSON PACIFIC CONSTRUCTION, INC., A PURPORTED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Peter Thompson, Toni Thompson And Thompson Pacific Constructions Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement (Ccp 664.6) To Declare June 9, 2015 Order Void Or, In The Alternative, To Reconsider That Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 19, 2015, Line 1, Defendants Peter Thompson, Toni Thompson And Thompson Pacific Construction's Motion To Enforce Settlement (CCP 664.6) To Declare June 9, 2015 Order Void Or, In The Alternative, To Reconsider That Order. GRANTED in part. The 2010 settlement agreement and the two separate stipulations for entry of judgment, although somewhat ambiguous, do appear to contemplate two separate judgments, one to be entered against TPCI at any time, and one to be entered against the individual Thompsons in the event they defaulted in the additional $70k they agreed to pay under the settlement. It does not appear that the parties intended the individual Thompsons' $70k payment to diminish or discharge the amount of the judgment against TPCI. Plaintiffs agree, however, that prejudgment interest is not recoverable per the settlement agreement. Accordingly, the Court will modify the judgment to reduce the amount owed to $326,691 plus 10% interest from the date of entry. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: GIAMPAULO BOSCHETTI et al VS. PACIFIC BAY INVESTMENTS, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 19, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF GIAMPAULO BOSCHETTI, PABO, LLC'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction. GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. A preliminary injunction requires a showing of great or irreparable harm; when that showing is made, the Court must then consider the likelihood that the moving party will ultimately prevail on the merits and the relative harm to the parties from issuance or nonissuance of the injunction. (Cosa Mesa City Employees' Assn v. City of Costa Mesa (2012) 209 Cal.App.4th 298, 306.) The purpose of a preliminary injunction "is to preserve the status quo until a final determination following a trial." (Id. at 305.) Here, the continued management of the property and deduction of management fees by Defendants is not the sort of great or irreparable injury which justifies a preliminary injunction. The fact that Boschetti waited over five years after the filing of this suit to bring his motion for preliminary injunction illustrates that this is not the sort of emergency situation in which judicial intervention is warranted. Boschetti does not allege that Defendants are insolvent or that monetary damages after trial will in any other way be in inadequate legal remedy. Furthermore, issuance of a preliminary injunction would leave the properties without any management; it would not further the purpose of "preserving the status quo until a final determination following a trial." (See Fretz v. Burke (1967) 247 Cal.App.2d 741, 746 (preliminary injunction requiring continued distributions of partnership income valid as preserving the status quo when defendants been illegally diverting the funds after litigation was commenced).) The Court also does not believe Plaintiff has shown a likelihood of success on the merits as to these claims. However, the Court GRANTS Plaintiff's request for a preliminary injunction immediately enjoining Defendants from using Plaintiff's share of partnership assets to pay for Defendants' legal defense in this action from this point forward. This admitted use of Plaintiff's share of the partnership assets to pay for the legal defense of his own lawsuit has brought appears to amount to misappropriation of partnership assets. (See Oakdale Village Group v. Fong (1996) 43 Cal.App.4th 539, 545-46.) This amounts to an irreparable injury which Plaintiff should not be forced to bear; Plaintiff is essentially being forced to bankroll both sides of his own litigation. Moreover, granting this portion of the preliminary injunction serves the purpose of preserving the status quo pending resolution of the claim. (See Cosa Mesa, supra, 209 Cal.App.4th at 305.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: BAY RIDGE PROPERTIES LLC VS. AMERICAN BROKERS CONDUIT CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Devon J. Bolla, As Trustee For The Broderick Street Trust Vs. Dated August 19, 2013s Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff In, Intervention And Cross-Defendant-In-Intervention Wells Fargo,
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 19, 2015, Line 1, Defendant-in-Intervention and Cross-Complainant-in-Intervention Devon J. Bolla, As Trustee For The Broderick Street Trust Vs. Dated August 19, 2013's Motion To Compel Plaintiff-In-Intervention And Cross-Defendant-In-Intervention Wells Fargo, National Association's Production of Documents and for Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Chuck Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties should be prepared to show what documents have already been produced relative to WFB's purchase of the loan, how voluminous the remaining documents sought are, and why redaction could not solve privacy concerns. Moving party should explain why what has been produced does not answer the question of whether WFB was lawful holder of the loan, and what other information would be contained in the further production that would assist it in is claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Paula Buckley, John Adams, Stephen Abrush, Linell Brandon And Tony Aumavae'S Motion For Order Quashing Deposition Subpoena For Sf Dept Of Public Health And Sf Homeless Outreach Team Is Transferred To Department 301 To Be Heard On August 19, 2015 At 9:00 A.M. Moving Party Is Ordered To Comply With Sflr 2.6b And Provide Courtesy Copy Of Reply To Department 301 No Later Than August 7, 2015 With A Cover Letter Reflecting New Hearing Date And Department.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 19, 2015, Line 2, Plaintiffs Paula Buckley, John Adams, Stephen Abrush, Linell Brandon, and Tony Aumavae's Motion For Order Quashing Deposition Subpoena Served Upon San Francisco Department of Public Health and SF Homeless Outreach Team. Pro Tem Judge Chuck Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The Court's tentative inclination is to grant the motion and require the defendants to re-issue more narrowly tailored subpoenas, as the present subpoena seeks all information pertaining to plaintiffs without any limitation. Parties should be prepared to discuss whether any plaintiffs have placed physical and/or mental condition in issue and if they have, the impact of Britt v. Superior Court and other cases limiting discovery to the physical/mental conditions that have been placed in issue. Another issue: does claiming "severe emotional distress," by itself, waive psychiatric privacy? Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARY O'SHEA ET AL VS. REAL BRIGHT MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Defendants Subpoena To Yahoo And Att Internet Services, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 19, 2015, Line 8, PLAINTIFF MARGIE BARR, DANIEL BARRETT, JASON BISHOP'S Motion To Quash Defendants Subpoena To Yahoo And Att Internet Services, And Request For Monetary Sanctions. The motion to quash is GRANTED in its entirety. Defendant argues it needs the information of six former plaintiffs and one who was never a party to help support its defense that the Plaintiffs did not actually click on the alleged spam links. The Court finds there is no rational reason to obtain this information in regard to people who are not actually parties to this litigation. (See CCP §2017.010.) As to the subpoenas related to current Plaintiffs, the court finds they are overbroad. Plaintifs request for damages for violation of the Stored Communications Act is DENIED. There is no cause of action in this case for violation under the SCA. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: ADON NETWORK, LLC VS. ONESCREEN, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Discovery Sanctions (The Only Remaining Issue Re: Plaintiff Adon Network Llc'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories, Set One And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And For Sanctions Against Defendant Onescreen, Inc.)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 19, 2015, Line 10, Motion For Discovery Sanctions (The Only Remaining Issue Re: Plaintiff Adon Network Llc'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories, Set One And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And For Sanctions Against Defendant Onescreen, Inc.) Request for sanctions DENIED. Plaintiff failed to comply with Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.040's requirement of "setting forth facts supporting the amount of any monetary sanction sought." It was not sufficient to merely request a dollar amount of sanctions, without explaining how that dollar amount was derived. Furthermore, the Court finds that Defendant acted with substantial justification in opposing the motion, because the opposition was based on its request for a protective order, a request which the Court granted. (See Foothill Properties v. Lyon/Copley Corona Associates (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 1542, 1558.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
